🖤 Between Pain and Misunderstanding… A Message from Gaza Could you please share my post, as it is important? What we are experiencing as people whose accounts have been verified from Gaza is not only about need, but also about a misunderstanding that has affected us all. The actions of a few individuals have reflected on everyone, leaving us facing deep embarrassment and intense emotional pain—without any intention or wrongdoing on our part. Having a donation link in the bio has unfortunately become a source of confusion, leading to reports and unfounded suspicions. However, we are grateful that there are people who manage these servers carefully, review cases, and ensure that we have not committed any abuse or violations. Still, we are sometimes subjected to harsh and unintended insults, being labeled as “beggars” or “scammers,” while in reality, those who are truly in need are often denied support because of actions that do not represent everyone. Please… stop overwhelming people here. Each person here evaluates cases and determines who truly deserves support in a fair and organized way. Do not overload their posts with repeated requests for help, and do not flood them with messages. Everyone here needs space, respect, and a balanced way of receiving support. If you see someone who has supported others, please do not contact them directly or pressure them, as this may lead them to stop helping altogether. I can endure days without food or medical care, but what I cannot endure is the feeling of being rejected or judged without reason. Unintentional offense is painful, yes… but I also understand that those who block or ignore often do so because of the constant pressure and repeated disturbance they face daily. Please do not embarrass those who helped create this space for us, and do not harm those who granted us verification and visibility. Finally, among us are educated and thoughtful people who are trying to build genuine human connections with others and prove themselves in professional spaces like Mastodon, which brings together respectful and intellectual communities. But this simple life we aspire to is weighed down by urgent need—the kind that remains constantly with us, as we try only to keep our families standing in the hardest of times. I am truly grateful to you all. ✒️ Nada #Gaza #Palestine

The arrest of Mohammad Hannoun has been annulled: he was accused of financing Hamas. The decision by the Appeals Court. The Court of Cassation has overturned the arrest of Mohammad Hannoun, the Palestinian activist and architect accused of financing Hamas through his Association for Solidarity with the Palestinian People (ASPSPP). The 64-year-old man had been arrested at the end of December along with six other people. Two of them, Raed El Salahat and Khalil Abu Deiah, had already been released while the arrests of Hannoun, Ra’ed Dawoud, Yaser Elasaly and Ryad Albunstanji were confirmed. For all four, the Court has deferred the decision to a section of the Appeals Court in Genoa. Pending the decision, Hannoun will remain in prison in Terni. The defense team said they were satisfied with the Court of Cassation’s decision. “Pending the motivations of the Supreme Court, it seems evident that the court will have to reconsider the positions of the investigated, for which only the sending of food aid and money intended for humanitarian activities in support of the civilian population was proven.” According to the lawyers, “it is also important that the Court of Cassation has declared inadmissible the appeal by the Public Prosecutor’s Office of Genoa, aimed at involving the documents transmitted by Israeli intelligence services, which had already been excluded by the Appeals Court.” Hannoun has lived in Genoa since 1983. The Prosecutor began to suspect possible links with Hamas at the end of the 1990s but the accusations were dropped. The Association’s activities had always been under surveillance and between 2021 and 2023, several banks and payment operators had frozen the accounts after some reports from the Israeli government. The foundation had already been accused in the past of organizing fundraising campaigns disguised as charitable activities for humanitarian purposes, but Hannoun has always denied it.
